title: Hawaii Five-O (1968 TV series) season 11

infobox.key: No. of episodes
Original network
infobox.value: [CBS](/wiki/CBS "CBS")

infobox.key: Original release
No. overall
infobox.value: No. in season | Title | Directed by | Written by | Original release date | Prod. code
1 | "The Sleeper" | Barry Crane | John Melson | September 28, 1978 (1978-09-28) | 1310-1729-0805

infobox.key: After a government agent is murdered, McGarrett must identify the traitor in a scientific facility. [Andrew Duggan](/wiki/Andrew_Duggan "Andrew Duggan") and [Steve Kanaly](/wiki/Steve_Kanaly "Steve Kanaly") guest stars.

infobox.value: 2 | "Horoscope for Murder" | Ralph Levy | Arthur Bernard Lewis | October 5, 1978 (1978-10-05) | 1310-1729-0806

infobox.key: When an astrologer ([Samantha Eggar](/wiki/Samantha_Eggar "Samantha Eggar")) visits McGarrett to inform him of a murderer with a terrible temper, his antagonism lessens when her assumptions seem to be partly true. [Tab Hunter](/wiki/Tab_Hunter "Tab Hunter") guest stars.

infobox.value: 3 | "Deadly Courier" | Reza Badiyi | Seeleg Lester and Sam Neuman | October 12, 1978 (1978-10-12) | 1310-1729-0809

infobox.key: While McGarrett follows a false lead, an undercover Danno is discovered and brainwashed to carry out the assassination of McGarrett. [Stephen Elliott](/wiki/Stephen_Elliott_(actor) "Stephen Elliott (actor)") and [Irene Yah-Ling Sun](/wiki/Irene_Yah-Ling_Sun "Irene Yah-Ling Sun") guest stars.

infobox.value: 4 | "The Case Against Philip Christie" | Dick Moder | Seeleg Lester | October 19, 1978 (1978-10-19) | 1310-1729-0802

infobox.key: McGarrett is the lone hold-out in a trial where the guilt of the accused seems certain. [Janis Paige](/wiki/Janis_Paige "Janis Paige"), [Lou Richards](/wiki/Lou_Richards_(actor) "Lou Richards (actor)") and [Eugene Lion](/wiki/Eugene_Lion "Eugene Lion") guest stars.

infobox.value: 5 | "Small Potatoes" | Reza Badiyi | Richard DeLong Adams | October 26, 1978 (1978-10-26) | 1310-1729-0812

infobox.key: Hired by a crime-syndicate chieftain, a jet-setter and his girlfriend scheme to discredit McGarrett. [Zohra Lampert](/wiki/Zohra_Lampert "Zohra Lampert") and [Richard Romanus](/wiki/Richard_Romanus "Richard Romanus") guest stars.

infobox.value: 6 | "A Distant Thunder" | Dennis Donnelly | Al Martinez | November 9, 1978 (1978-11-09) | 1310-1729-0801

infobox.key: Danny goes undercover in a neo-Nazi organization to investigate a violent hate campaign directed against a congressional hopeful. [James Olson](/wiki/James_Olson_(actor) "James Olson (actor)") guest stars.

infobox.value: 7 | "Death Mask" | Ralph Levy | Robert I. Holt | November 16, 1978 (1978-11-16) | 1310-1729-0808
8 | "The Pagoda Factor" | Dennis Donnelly | *S* : Irv Pearlberg *S/T* : Al Martinez | November 23, 1978 (1978-11-23) | 1310-1729-0807

infobox.key: McGarrett engineers a convict's ([Brian Tochi](/wiki/Brian_Tochi "Brian Tochi")) escape so he can rejoin his gang and provide information about their activities. [Dane Clark](/wiki/Dane_Clark "Dane Clark") guest stars.

infobox.value: 9 | "A Long Time Ago" | Robert L. Morrison | Arthur Bernard Lewis | November 30, 1978 (1978-11-30) | 1310-1729-0810

infobox.key: Danno is stunned to learn that a former flame ([Katherine Cannon](/wiki/Katherine_Cannon "Katherine Cannon")) is apparently a shoplifter and a fugitive's ([Burr DeBenning](/wiki/Burr_DeBenning "Burr DeBenning")) girlfriend.

infobox.value: 10 | "Why Won't Linda Die?" | Jack Lord | Ken Pettus | December 14, 1978 (1978-12-14) | 1310-1729-0816

infobox.key: The death of a prominent politician reveals a strange love triangle. [Sharon Farrell](/wiki/Sharon_Farrell "Sharon Farrell") guest stars.

infobox.value: 11 | "The Miracle Man" | Lawrence Dobkin | Robert Janes | December 21, 1978 (1978-12-21) | 1310-1729-0817

infobox.key: An evangelist ([Keith Baxter](/wiki/Keith_Baxter_(actor) "Keith Baxter (actor)")) is assaulted by a man who claims the preacher is responsible for his wife's death. [James Sikking](/wiki/James_Sikking "James Sikking") and [Jean Marsh](/wiki/Jean_Marsh "Jean Marsh") guest star.

infobox.value: 12 | "Number One with a Bullet" | Don Weis | Robert Janes | December 28, 1978 (1978-12-28) | 1310-1729-0813
14 | "The Meighan Conspiracy" | Robert L. Morrison | Seeleg Lester | January 18, 1979 (1979-01-18) | 1310-1729-0819

infobox.key: When a robbed bank vault shows no signs of break-in, an inside job seems the likely solution. McGarrett's trail of clues leads him back to the builder ([Robert Reed](/wiki/Robert_Reed "Robert Reed")) of the vault and his wife ([Barbara Anderson](/wiki/Barbara_Anderson_(actress) "Barbara Anderson (actress)")).

infobox.value: 15 | "The Spirit Is Willie" | Reza Badiyi | *S* : Sam Neuman *S/T* : Seeleg Lester | January 25, 1979 (1979-01-25) | 1310-1729-0821

infobox.key: Millicent Shand ([Mildred Natwick](/wiki/Mildred_Natwick "Mildred Natwick")) returns and claims that her niece's ([Diana Scarwid](/wiki/Diana_Scarwid "Diana Scarwid")) husband has faked his own death in order to get his hands on $500,000. [Robert Vaughn](/wiki/Robert_Vaughn "Robert Vaughn") and [Eduard Franz](/wiki/Eduard_Franz "Eduard Franz") guest stars.

infobox.value: 16 | "The Bark and the Bite" | Don Weis | *S* : Shelly Mitchell *S/T* : Richard deRoy | February 8, 1979 (1979-02-08) | 1310-1729-0815

infobox.key: A vacationing heiress ([Tricia O'Neil](/wiki/Tricia_O%27Neil "Tricia O'Neil")) seems unconcerned when McGarrett warns her that someone ([John Saxon](/wiki/John_Saxon "John Saxon")) is after her ruby. [Nita Talbot](/wiki/Nita_Talbot "Nita Talbot") guest stars.

infobox.value: 17 | "Stringer" | Ray Austin | *S* : Paul Williams *S/T* : Robert Janes | February 22, 1979 (1979-02-22) | 1310-1729-0822

infobox.key: A shady photographer ([Paul Williams](/wiki/Paul_Williams_(songwriter) "Paul Williams (songwriter)")) becomes involved with gangsters. [Sandra Kerns](/wiki/Sandra_Kerns "Sandra Kerns"), [Ross Martin](/wiki/Ross_Martin "Ross Martin") and [Robert Clarke](/wiki/Robert_Clarke "Robert Clarke") guest stars.

infobox.value: 18 | "The Execution File" | Don Weis | Don Balluck | March 1, 1979 (1979-03-01) | 1310-1729-0824

infobox.key: A private detective ([Robert Loggia](/wiki/Robert_Loggia "Robert Loggia")) is rescuing girls from prostitution, and using the opportunity to kill the pimps as well. [John Larch](/wiki/John_Larch "John Larch") and [Kaki Hunter](/wiki/Kaki_Hunter "Kaki Hunter") guest stars.

infobox.value: 19 | "A Very Personal Matter" | Harry F. Hogan III | Robert Janes | March 15, 1979 (1979-03-15) | 1310-1729-0811

infobox.key: McGarrett must protect a physician ([Fritz Weaver](/wiki/Fritz_Weaver "Fritz Weaver")) suspected of drug peddling from a grieving father ([Cameron Mitchell](/wiki/Cameron_Mitchell_(actor) "Cameron Mitchell (actor)")) whose son died of a drug overdose. [Simone Griffeth](/wiki/Simone_Griffeth "Simone Griffeth") guest stars.

infobox.value: 20 | "The Skyline Killer" | Beau Van den Ecker | Robert Janes | March 22, 1979 (1979-03-22) | 1310-1729-0823

infobox.key: McGarrett and a profit-seeking writer ([Charles Cioffi](/wiki/Charles_Cioffi "Charles Cioffi")) compete to find a maniacal killer.

infobox.value: 21 | "The Year of the Horse" | Don Weis | Richard DeLong Adams | April 5, 1979 (1979-04-05) | 1310-1729-0803

infobox.key: Characters
infobox.value: * [2010](/wiki/List_of_Hawaii_Five-0_(2010_TV_series)_characters "List of Hawaii Five-0 (2010 TV series) characters") * [Steve McGarrett](/wiki/Steve_McGarrett "Steve McGarrett") * [Danny "Danno" Williams](/wiki/Danny_%22Danno%22_Williams "Danny \"Danno\" Williams")

infobox.value: Laura Sode-Matteson begins her regular series appearance as McGarrett's secretary, Luana. | | | | | | |

This is a list of episodes from the eleventh season of *[Hawaii Five-O](/wiki/Hawaii_Five-O_(1968_TV_series) "Hawaii Five-O (1968 TV series)")*. This season marks the final series appearance of [James MacArthur](/wiki/James_MacArthur "James MacArthur").

## Broadcast history

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Hawaii_Five-O_(1968_TV_series)_season_11&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Broadcast history")]

The season originally aired Thursdays at 9:00-10:00 pm (EST).[[1]](#cite_note-1)[[2]](#cite_note-2)

## DVD release

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Hawaii_Five-O_(1968_TV_series)_season_11&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: DVD release")]

The season was released on DVD by Paramount Home Video.

## Episodes

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Hawaii_Five-O_(1968_TV_series)_season_11&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Episodes")]

See also: [List of Hawaii Five-O (1968 TV series) episodes](/wiki/List_of_Hawaii_Five-O_(1968_TV_series)_episodes "List of Hawaii Five-O (1968 TV series) episodes")

[Robert Vaughn](/wiki/Robert_Vaughn "Robert Vaughn") and [Eduard Franz](/wiki/Eduard_Franz "Eduard Franz") guest stars. | | | | | | |
| 257 | 16 | "The Bark and the Bite" | Don Weis | *S* : Shelly Mitchell *S/T* : Richard deRoy | February 8, 1979 (1979-02-08) | 1310-1729-0815 |
| A vacationing heiress ([Tricia O'Neil](/wiki/Tricia_O%27Neil "Tricia O'Neil")) seems unconcerned when McGarrett warns her that someone ([John Saxon](/wiki/John_Saxon "John Saxon")) is after her ruby. [Nita Talbot](/wiki/Nita_Talbot "Nita Talbot") guest stars. | | | | | | |
| 258 | 17 | "Stringer" | Ray Austin | *S* : Paul Williams *S/T* : Robert Janes | February 22, 1979 (1979-02-22) | 1310-1729-0822 |
| A shady photographer ([Paul Williams](/wiki/Paul_Williams_(songwriter) "Paul Williams (songwriter)")) becomes involved with gangsters. [Sandra Kerns](/wiki/Sandra_Kerns "Sandra Kerns"), [Ross Martin](/wiki/Ross_Martin "Ross Martin") and [Robert Clarke](/wiki/Robert_Clarke "Robert Clarke") guest stars. | | | | | | |
| 259 | 18 | "The Execution File" | Don Weis | Don Balluck | March 1, 1979 (1979-03-01) | 1310-1729-0824 |
| A private detective ([Robert Loggia](/wiki/Robert_Loggia "Robert Loggia")) is rescuing girls from prostitution, and using the opportunity to kill the pimps as well. [John Larch](/wiki/John_Larch "John Larch") and [Kaki Hunter](/wiki/Kaki_Hunter "Kaki Hunter") guest stars. | | | | | | |
| 260 | 19 | "A Very Personal Matter" | Harry F. Hogan III | Robert Janes | March 15, 1979 (1979-03-15) | 1310-1729-0811 |
| McGarrett must protect a physician ([Fritz Weaver](/wiki/Fritz_Weaver "Fritz Weaver")) suspected of drug peddling from a grieving father ([Cameron Mitchell](/wiki/Cameron_Mitchell_(actor) "Cameron Mitchell (actor)")) whose son died of a drug overdose. [Simone Griffeth](/wiki/Simone_Griffeth "Simone Griffeth") guest stars. | | | | | | |
| 261 | 20 | "The Skyline Killer" | Beau Van den Ecker | Robert Janes | March 22, 1979 (1979-03-22) | 1310-1729-0823 |
| McGarrett and a profit-seeking writer ([Charles Cioffi](/wiki/Charles_Cioffi "Charles Cioffi")) compete to find a maniacal killer. | | | | | | |
| 262 | 21 | "The Year of the Horse" | Don Weis | Richard DeLong Adams | April 5, 1979 (1979-04-05) | 1310-1729-0803 |
| 263 | 22 |
|
| An Asian dies on a flight from Bangkok to Honolulu when a bag of heroin in her stomach bursts; the trail leads McGarrett and Danny to a Singapore drug ring. [Barry Bostwick](/wiki/Barry_Bostwick "Barry Bostwick"), [Victoria Principal](/wiki/Victoria_Principal "Victoria Principal") and [George Lazenby](/wiki/George_Lazenby "George Lazenby") guest stars. **NOTE:** This feature-length episode was later shown in two parts in syndication (it was also produced early in the season - in fact, it was filmed before the season premiere - but held back). | | | | | | | | | | | |
